headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Oxford Instruments repurchased 33,608 shares at 28.06-30.28p during week of 17-23 July under existing buyback programme.
summary: Oxford Instruments executed share purchases totalling 33,608 ordinary shares between 17-23 July 2026 via JP Morgan Securities on the London Stock Exchange, at prices ranging from 28.06p to 30.28p per share. Volume-weighted average prices ranged from 28.35p to 30.12p daily. Following settlement and cancellation, total issued shares reduced to 55,000,134 with no treasury shares held. The purchases form part of the buyback programme announced 2 March 2026.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within announced programme; demonstrates capital return to shareholders via cancellation. No material news content; standard market procedure with transparent execution pricing disclosed.

headline: Holding in Company
brief: Principal Global Investors falls below 4% threshold in AIB Group, triggering regulatory notification.
summary: Principal Global Investors, LLC, through its parent Principal Financial Group, crossed below the 4% regulatory threshold in AIB Group PLC on 21 July 2026, notified 23 July. The entity now holds 3.99% of voting rights (83.34m shares) via indirect holdings, down from 4.00% previously. PGI is a registered US investment adviser managing these shares on behalf of various clients with discretionary voting power.
retail_take: Routine disclosure reflecting a minor shareholding reduction; no material strategic implications for the bank or significant market catalyst expected.

headline: Municipality Finance issues EUR 25 million notes under its MTN programme
Post Stabilisation Notice
brief: HSBC confirms no stabilisation activity on Iberdrola's €750m dual-tranche bond offering.
summary: HSBC Bank plc, acting as Stabilising Manager, confirms no stabilisation was undertaken for Iberdrola Finanzas' €750m bond issuance comprising two tranches: €750m of 3.125% notes due June 2030 offered at 99.833, and €750m of 3.75% notes due June 2036 offered at 99.705. The announcement is a routine post-issuance notice confirming market-making activity parameters.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure indicating the bond offering completed without requiring price support; suggests adequate demand. Immaterial for retail investors; standard post-issuance procedure.

headline: Post Stabilisation Notice
brief: HSBC confirms no stabilisation activity occurred during Iberdrola's EUR 1.5bn bond offering.
summary: HSBC Bank plc acted as Stabilising Manager for Iberdrola Finanzas' dual-tranche bond issuance totalling EUR 750m each: a 3.125% bond due June 2030 priced at 99.833, and a 3.75% bond due June 2036 priced at 99.705. The notice confirms no stabilisation trading was undertaken during the offer period, a standard post-offering disclosure indicating market conditions did not require price support mechanisms.
retail_take: Routine post-issuance disclosure with no market implications; confirms orderly bond placement without need for stabilisation intervention, suggesting adequate investor demand.

headline: Notice Regarding US Patent Application
brief: Quantum Blockchain Technologies receives US patent allowance notice for ASIC Ultra Boost cryptocurrency mining technology.
summary: Quantum Blockchain Technologies (AIM: QBT) has received a Notice of Allowance from the US Patent and Trademark Office for patent application 18/696,073, titled "Message Scheduling for Cryptographic Hashing" (internally known as "ASIC Ultra Boost"). The company is confirming details with UK and US patent attorneys and will provide further updates. The development indicates progress in the company's R&D programme focused on developing advanced Bitcoin mining tools and techniques.
retail_take: Patent allowances are typically positive signals for tech-focused investors as they validate intellectual property and competitive advantage, though full market impact depends on commercialisation timing and competitive landscape.

headline: Correction: Merger and Financing
brief: Scancell corrects pro forma net cash to £59.2m and Private Placement gross proceeds to $39.1m in merger financing announcement.
summary: Scancell corrects two figures in its 23 July merger announcement with Neuphoria Therapeutics: pro forma net cash balance revised to £59.2m ($79.1m) and Private Placement gross proceeds confirmed at $39.1m (£29.2m). The all-share merger creates combined company listed on Nasdaq with Scancell shareholders owning 85.5%, Neuphoria 14.5%. Financing includes $39.1m private placement, ~$15m UK raising, and up to $25m BlackRock debt. Expected completion Q4 2026 pending shareholder approvals. Combined entity will focus on iSCIB1+ Phase 3 trial in melanoma with fast-track FDA designation.
retail_take: Technical correction with no material change to transaction fundamentals; sufficient funding secured and cash position clarified, supporting execution of lead clinical programme through 2028-29 milestones.

headline: Future retirement of Fund Manager
brief: Lowland Investment Co's long-serving fund manager James Henderson to retire June 2027 after 36 years; Laura Foll takes over.
summary: James Henderson, Fund Manager of Lowland Investment Company plc for 36 years, will retire in June 2027 after 44 years in financial services. Laura Foll, who has managed the fund since 2016 and brings 17 years of industry experience, will assume full responsibility. Henderson will work closely with the team during the transition period. No changes to the Company's investment objective or strategy are planned.
retail_take: Orderly succession with internal continuity likely viewed positively; Laura Foll's decade-long track record and Board confidence suggest minimal disruption risk for investors.

headline: Trading Update & Quarterly Results
brief: Taylor Maritime announces $45m third capital redemption and Q2 results as managed wind-down progresses with five remaining vessels.
summary: Taylor Maritime completed its third compulsory share redemption of $45m (85.83 cents per share) in July, bringing total shareholder returns to $218.4m ($1.10 per share since IPO). Q2 net profit was $1.5m on $9.0m charter revenue with $13,433 daily TCE earnings. Fleet reduced to 5 vessels (average age 12.4 years) worth $88.9m after divesting one Ultramax and exiting a 50% JV stake for $28m combined proceeds. NAV declined to $122.2m (85.20 cents per share) from $154.3m as cash decreased to $44.7m from $72.0m. Company targets substantially returning NAV by year-end and plans delisting following final vessel sales.
retail_take: Managed wind-down proceeding as planned with substantial capital returns; flat TCE performance offset by fleet shrinkage. Delisting signals endgame for closed-ended investment fund; execution risk remains on remaining vessel disposals in current market conditions.

headline: Wise US national trust bank charter application
brief: OCC denies Wise's US national trust bank charter application; company plans resubmission under new GENIUS Act framework.
summary: The US Office of the Comptroller of the Currency rejected Wise's national trust bank charter application submitted over a year ago, citing historical compliance issues the company has since addressed. The rejection was influenced by a July 2025 Multi-State Consent Order and changing regulatory conditions, including the Federal Reserve's May 2026 pause on account access for uninsured trust banks, which undermined the original application's viability. Wise maintains 80+ licenses globally and 48-state money transmitter licenses; the denial does not affect existing operations. The company plans to submit a new application under the GENIUS Act framework.
retail_take: Setback for Wise's US banking ambitions but operationally immaterial given existing licenses. Market likely views this as negative near-term optics but manageable given the company's scale ($240bn annual transaction volume) and regulatory flexibility to reapply.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: HarbourVest repurchases 7,000 shares at £33.28 each for cancellation under AGM authority.
summary: HarbourVest Global Private Equity Limited purchased 7,000 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 at an average price of £33.2801 per share for cancellation, acting under authority granted at its 15 July 2026 AGM. Following this transaction, the company has 69,590,352 shares in issue. This figure serves as the denominator for FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules notifications.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution; modest scale suggests ongoing capital management rather than material buyback programme; minimal market-moving significance.

headline: Amendment to a Dragones Share Purchase Agreement
brief: Galantas amends Dragones share purchase agreement, accelerating final payment from 2029 to 2028 and becoming joint guarantor.
summary: Galantas and subsidiary OXI amended the share purchase agreement with Luis Catril regarding the Dragones acquisition. US$5.0m was paid immediately, with US$9.0m due by April 25, 2027. Galantas is now guarantor and joint co-debtor for remaining payments totaling US$22.0m, with final payment accelerated to December 31, 2028. Total Dragones consideration is US$31m, with 91,313,890 Galantas shares issued. Catril released all disputes regarding share consideration.
retail_take: The acceleration of payment obligations increases near-term liquidity pressure and default risk if OXI cannot fund payments, potentially diluting shareholder value through forced capital raises or asset sales.

headline: Proposed Aftan Shareholder Return Programme
brief: Kazera Global to return approximately 80% of US$10.5m Aftan settlement proceeds to qualifying shareholders via contingent value rights.
summary: Kazera will establish a shareholder return programme distributing roughly 80% of net cash from its US$10.5m Aftan settlement to qualifying shareholders holding shares on 3 August 2026 record date. Distributions occur via contingent value rights (CVRs), preserving entitlements independently of subsequent share ownership. Two scenarios: single distribution in early 2027 if Hebei settles early at US$9m by December 2026, or three distributions in 2028-2030 if instalments continue. Retained 20% strengthens balance sheet and funds growth, particularly the South African heavy mineral sands asset with SAI.
retail_take: Positive signal: demonstrates value realisation from legacy Aftan dispute and disciplined capital allocation, rewarding long-term shareholders while retaining growth capital; CVR structure ensures fairness and clarity for existing shareholders versus new investors.

headline: EBT Share Dealing
brief: Mission Group's EBT acquires 458,743 shares at 19.0p per share, bringing total holding to 0.9%.
summary: The Mission Group plc's Employee Benefit Trust purchased 458,743 ordinary shares of 10 pence each at 19.0 pence per share on 23 July 2026. Following this acquisition, the EBT holds a total of 781,747 ordinary shares, representing approximately 0.9% of the company's voting rights. The purchase signals continued confidence in the Group's value and supports employee share ownership.
retail_take: Routine EBT share acquisition showing management confidence; modest holding increase unlikely to move markets materially but demonstrates ongoing employee engagement strategy.

headline: New Corporate Presentation
brief: Gunsynd PLC publishes updated corporate presentation for Barb Gold Project in Manitoba with high-grade surface samples.
summary: Gunsynd (AIM: GUN) released an updated presentation detailing exploration results from its Barb Gold Project in Manitoba's Rice Lake Belt. Phase 1 results include grab samples up to 489 g/t Au at the Lotus prospect, with visible gold confirmed across 12 samples. The company has completed prospecting, geological mapping, and a property-wide UAV magnetic survey. Lotus is identified as the priority drill target, with historic production of 8,287 tonnes at 4.56 g/t Au milled in 1982. The consolidated ~6,612 hectare property encompasses three occurrences on the same structural corridor.
retail_take: Presentation updates validate geological model with tangible high-grade surface samples and defined drill target, but grab samples are selective and not representative of economic grade; market likely reads as positive early-stage exploration news supporting upcoming drilling campaign.

headline: Change of Auditor
brief: Hercules PLC appoints Cooper Parry Group as new auditor following S&W Partners' resignation.
summary: Hercules plc has completed a formal tender process and appointed Cooper Parry Group Ltd as its external auditor, replacing S&W Partners Audit Limited. S&W Partners has formally resigned without notifying the Company of any reasons or matters that shareholders or creditors should know about. The change follows a competitive auditor selection process.
retail_take: Routine auditor change following formal tender process; no adverse matters disclosed. Market likely views as non-event unless resignation context later emerges.

headline: Change of Company Secretary
brief: Synergia Energy appoints Stacey Britza as Company Secretary, effective immediately, replacing Allison Pacinotti.
summary: Stacey Britza, who joined Synergia Energy as Non-Executive Director on 12 May 2026, has been promoted to Company Secretary with immediate effect. Britza has served the company for 18 years as Executive Assistant and Assistant Company Secretary, providing extensive involvement in governance, regulatory compliance and Board administration. She replaces Allison Pacinotti, whose contributions the Board acknowledged.
retail_take: Internal promotion of long-serving employee to routine administrative role; demonstrates governance continuity and succession planning, likely viewed as non-event by markets.

headline: Notice of Results
brief: Chesnara to report H1 2026 interim results on 25 August with analyst presentation.
summary: Chesnara plc, the FTSE 250 European life insurance and pensions consolidator, will publish its interim results for the half year ended 30 June 2026 on Tuesday 25 August 2026. An analyst presentation will be held at 9.30am at RBC Capital Markets in London, with online access available. The company administers approximately 1.4 million policies across UK, Netherlands and Swedish operations and has delivered 21 consecutive years of dividend increases.
retail_take: Routine results announcement with no material new information; market will focus on operational performance, capital position and dividend trajectory at the August results event.

headline: Revised Notice of AGM
brief: Firering reschedules AGM to 14 September 2026, requests shareholders resubmit proxy forms.
summary: Firering Strategic Minerals PLC (AIM: FRG) has rescheduled its Annual General Meeting to 10:00 a.m. BST on 14 September 2026 at Hill Dickinson LLP in London. Shareholders who previously submitted proxy forms for the original AGM must complete and return new proxy forms per the revised Notice of AGM. The lime producer and critical minerals explorer continues ramping up its Limeco quicklime project in Zambia (45% stake), targeting 600-800 tonnes daily production for regional copper producers.
retail_take: Routine administrative rescheduling of AGM with no material business impact disclosed; shareholders must take action to resubmit proxies or risk losing voting rights.

headline: Updated Corporate Presentation
brief: Beowulf Mining updates corporate presentation on investor website with latest company materials and strategy.
summary: Beowulf Mining PLC has published an updated corporate presentation, available via its investor relations website. The Q3 2026 presentation is the latest addition to the company's reports and presentations library, which includes quarterly financial results, annual reports, and historical investor materials spanning back to 2014. The announcement includes standard forward-looking statements highlighting risks including financing needs, geological changes, regulatory shifts, and iron ore price volatility.
retail_take: Routine disclosure with no material new information; market likely treats as non-event unless presentation contains significant updates to project timelines or financing status.

headline: AOTI achieves transformational milestone
brief: AOTI achieves major US Medicare coverage milestone for TWO2® topical oxygen therapy treating diabetic foot ulcers.
summary: CMS issued a proposed Local Coverage Determination affirming topical oxygen therapy as reasonable and necessary for diabetic foot ulcers failing four weeks of optimized care. The final LCD, expected within one year after a 45-day comment period, will mandate Medicare coverage and reimbursement for AOTI's TWO2® therapy, providing access to approximately 69 million Medicare enrollees. AOTI holds 75% market share in topical oxygen with existing infrastructure covering 26 US states (86% of population). The company targets a $400m near-to-medium-term revenue opportunity with potential $26bn addressable market as coverage expands.
retail_take: Market should read this positively as transformational de-risking of reimbursement, validating product differentiation, and unlocking substantial revenue from the largest US payer segment without requiring major sales infrastructure investment.

headline: Kepler Trust Intelligence: New Research
brief: Kepler Trust Intelligence publishes new research on Schroder Income Growth Fund's 30-year dividend growth track record.
summary: Kepler Partners has released investment bank-quality research on Schroder Income Growth (SCF), highlighting the trust's three decades of real dividend growth since 1996, compounding at 4.1% annually versus 2.5% inflation. Under manager Sue Noffke since July 2011, the fund delivered 14.9% NAV total return over the past year to 20 July 2026, though underperformed the FTSE All-Share's 19.6% due to overweight small/mid-cap exposure. The trust trades at a 5.7% discount with 4.1% yield, 0.78% ongoing charge, and 9.5% net gearing; the board intends to pursue a 31st consecutive year of dividend growth.
retail_take: Non-event marketing communication. Research underscores long-term income credentials and experienced management but notes near-term underperformance and small-cap sensitivity risks. Market likely neutral; highlights structural appeal without material news.

headline: Broker Appointment & Successful £850k Fundraise
brief: Hydrogen Utopia raises £850k at 2.0p/share and appoints Clear Capital Markets as broker.
summary: Hydrogen Utopia International (LSE: HUI) completed an £850,000 fundraise via 42.5 million new ordinary shares at 2.0p per share. Clear Capital Markets appointed as sole broker with immediate effect. Net proceeds will fund Saudi Arabia expansion, advance Fortress Fuels initiative, and extend Inentec licence agreements. Post-admission, company will have 475.1 million shares outstanding. Admission expected 30 July 2026.
retail_take: Capital raise at 2.0p supports strategic expansion into high-value sectors (SAF, JP8) amid elevated energy prices, though significant share dilution and modest fundraise size suggest early-stage funding challenges.

headline: Statement Re Cameroon Mining Arbitration Matter
brief: ICC arbitration tribunal awards $600m+ to Burford's counterparty in Cameroon mining case; Burford entitled to AUD$250m+ if paid.
summary: An ICC International Court of Arbitration tribunal issued an award exceeding $600m in favor of Burford's counterparty in a Cameroon mining arbitration matter. If fully paid, Burford's entitlement would exceed AUD$250m, accruing entirely to the balance sheet with no participation from investment funds. However, the award remains subject to significant litigation and collection risks, including potential annulment, set-aside proceedings, and enforcement challenges across jurisdictions. Ultimate recovery timing and amount remain uncertain, and settlement could occur at substantially lower values.
retail_take: Positive headline with material caveats; while AUD$250m+ upside is substantial, recovery is uncertain and subject to post-award proceedings, collection risks, and potential settlement discounts. Market likely sees optionality value but tempered by execution risks.

headline: Operational Update
brief: Total Graphite appoints experienced mining engineer Graeme Chester as Madagascar Head of Operations to oversee production restart targeting January 2027.
summary: Total Graphite has appointed Graeme Chester, a mining engineer with 50 years' experience in Africa, Australia and Asia, as Head of Operations in Madagascar. Chester will oversee operational optimisation and readiness at the Vatomina project ahead of production restart targeting above 1,000 tonnes per month from January 2027. Diamond drilling campaign is ongoing with seven holes completed to date; assay results pending. The project has an exploration target of 18-20Mt at 4% graphitic content plus existing 6Mt resource at 3.8%.
retail_take: Appointment of experienced turnaround specialist with clear January 2027 production timeline suggests management confidence in operational readiness and signals disciplined execution. Positive signal for recovery trajectory, though execution risk remains until production restart achieved.

headline: Preliminary Results
brief: Victoria PLC reports FY2026 results showing revenue decline to £1,045.5m but operational progress amid refinancing wins.
summary: Victoria's year to 28 March 2026 saw underlying revenue fall 6.3% to £1,045.5m with underlying EBITDA declining to £92.3m (margin 8.8%). Statutory net loss after tax was £326.3m, driven by asset impairments and refinancing costs. However, Q1 FY2027 showed encouraging 7% revenue growth. The company successfully refinanced 2026 debt in August 2025 and announced 8 July that 90%+ of 2028 bond holders backed a refinancing extending maturities to 2031, reducing liabilities by ~£300m and finance costs by ~£34m annually. FY2027 EBITDA guidance: at least £115m.
retail_take: Market likely to focus on near-term debt relief and Q1 recovery signals offsetting persistent FY2026 weakness; refinancing risk mitigation is material positive, though macro headwinds persist.

headline: LOS PELAMBRES OPERATIONAL UPDATE
brief: Los Pelambres mine resumes operations after weather-related shutdown; full-year guidance unchanged.
summary: Antofagasta's Los Pelambres operation shut down orderly on 18 July following intense precipitation and power outages affecting Chilean regions. Operations have resumed as of 23 July with no safety incidents or material equipment damage reported. The El Mauro tailings dam and water management systems functioned as designed. Full-year production guidance remains unchanged; further details will be provided in Half Year Results on 13 August.
retail_take: Market likely views this as contained, temporary disruption given swift resumption, no infrastructure damage, and maintained guidance, positioning this as a non-event operationally.

headline: Admission to Trading
brief: Volex PLC upgrades from AIM to Main Market of London Stock Exchange effective 24 July 2026.
summary: Volex plc's ordinary shares were admitted to the Official List and Main Market of the LSE at 8:00 a.m. on 24 July 2026, with simultaneous cancellation from AIM trading. The company has 184,377,640 issued ordinary shares outstanding with ISIN GB0009390070 and ticker VLX. CEO Nat Rothschild stated the move reflects business growth and scale, broadening appeal to UK and international investors while maintaining the company's strategy execution.
retail_take: Main Market upgrade signals confidence in company maturity and growth trajectory; typically seen as positive validation, though no new capital raised and existing shareholders unaffected mechanically.

headline: Operational Update: From Vision to Execution
brief: Amigo Resources transitions to gold production execution at two Tanzania processing centres with initial 20kg monthly output targets.
summary: Amigo Resources has completed infrastructure development at Mojimoto and Kabete processing centres in Tanzania following PCL grant approval. Operational milestones include completed water/power infrastructure, 6,000 tonnes of historical tailings relocated, and approximately 1 million tonnes identified for reprocessing. Two pilot facilities designed for 200,000 tonnes annual capacity each are now commissioning, with target production of ~20kg gold monthly per plant. Parallel exploration continues at Big Mine via micro-seismic surveys and trenching programmes. Management flagged preliminary evaluation of potential RTO to accelerate growth.
retail_take: Market likely reads positively: transition from exploration to tangible production with completed infrastructure and stated output targets demonstrates execution capability. RTO hint suggests growth ambitions. However, modest 20kg/month production scales and preliminary exploration status may temper enthusiasm.

headline: Admission to Trading
brief: Capricorn Energy admits 845,313 new ordinary shares to LSE Main Market, bringing total issued shares to 71.4m.
summary: Capricorn Energy PLC admitted 845,313 ordinary shares (799/122 pence par value) to trading on London Stock Exchange Main Market on 23 July 2026, expanding total issued shares from approximately 70.6m to 71.4m shares. The new shares are fully fungible with existing ordinary shares. Capricorn is a cash flow-focused energy producer operating onshore assets in Egypt's Western Desert, reporting FY2025 net cash of US$103m and oil & gas revenue of US$134m, with current share price at 375 GBX.
retail_take: Routine capital admission announcement with modest share issuance (~1.2% dilution). Non-event for market; announcement processed on admission date with no prospectus required, suggesting straightforward capital management.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Emma Sibley, spouse of Non-executive Director Earl Sibley, purchased 10,000 Henry Boot PLC shares at £1.54 on 23 July 2026.
summary: Emma Sibley, person closely associated with Non-executive Director Earl Sibley, purchased 10,000 ordinary shares of Henry Boot PLC on 23 July 2026 at £1.54 per share, totalling £15,400. The transaction occurred on the London Stock Exchange. This is an initial notification disclosure under EU Market Abuse Regulation requirements.
retail_take: Routine insider transaction disclosure; modest £15,400 purchase by director-linked party suggests confidence but represents non-material shareholding activity.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: David Zwirn, associate of Non-Executive Director Claudine Collins, purchases 51,546 MISSION Group shares at 19.39p.
summary: On 23 July 2026, David Zwirn, a person closely associated with Non-Executive Director Claudine Collins, purchased 51,546 ordinary shares in The MISSION Group plc (AIM: TMG) at 19.39p per share on the London Stock Exchange. This transaction increased Zwirn's beneficial holding to 195,546 ordinary shares. The purchase represents a director-linked shareholding disclosure under UK Market Abuse Regulation 596/2014.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a director-related share purchase; demonstrates insider confidence but modest scale suggests non-material position-building rather than significant commitment signal.

headline: Board Change
brief: Comptoir Group's Finance Director James Fisher to step down by October 30, 2026; Head of Finance Nathan Portelli to succeed him.
summary: James Fisher is stepping down as Finance Director of Comptoir Group PLC (AIM: COM), a 27-restaurant Lebanese and Middle Eastern dining operator. He will remain until October 30, 2026 to oversee an orderly handover. Nathan Portelli, currently Head of Finance, will assume the Finance Director role later in 2026 and will work closely with Fisher during the transition period.
retail_take: Routine management succession with internal promotion; planned transition minimises disruption risk. Likely viewed as orderly and low-impact unless Portelli's inexperience at board level raises concerns.

headline: Investor Update H1 2026
brief: Macau Property Opportunities Fund reports H1 2026 NAV down 17.6% to US$0.38/share amid continued luxury property market decline and accelerated asset sales.
summary: MPO's adjusted NAV per share fell 17.6% to US$0.38 (29p) as of 30 June 2026, with portfolio valuation down 7% to US$61.3m. The luxury residential market—core of the portfolio—experienced an 18% year-on-year price decline in Q1 2026, extending seven consecutive quarters of declines (31% cumulative drop vs Q1 2024). Over 90% of The Waterside units are sold; Fountainside faces regulatory delays; and Penha Heights is actively marketed. With US$34.1m debt and failed December 2026 capital raise, lenders have forced accelerated divestment, potentially realizing values materially below stated NAV.
retail_take: Significant deterioration in asset values and liquidity constraints suggest further downsides for shareholders; accelerated sales under duress risk realizing well below valuations, with substantial discount-to-NAV (78.6%) already reflecting distressed positioning.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Premier Miton Group's voting stake in Conygar Investment Company falls to 9.21% from 10.55%.
summary: Premier Miton Group plc reduced its holding in The Conygar Investment Company PLC to 9.21% of voting rights (5,492,901 shares) as of 22 July 2026, down from 10.55% previously. The threshold crossing was notified to the issuer on 23 July 2026. The reduction represents a disposal of voting rights, bringing Premier Miton below the 10% disclosure threshold. No financial instruments are involved in the holding.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a major shareholding reduction. The fall below 10% may indicate a modest portfolio rebalancing with minimal strategic implications for either party.

headline: HSBC TO SELL SGP H&L INSURANCE BUSINESS TO ALLIANZ
brief: HSBC to sell Singapore life and health insurance business to Allianz for S$2.7bn, completing H1 2027.
summary: HSBC has agreed to sell HSBC Life (Singapore) Pte. Ltd. to Allianz Asia Holdings for S$2.7 billion (US$2.1 billion), with completion expected in the first half of 2027 subject to regulatory approval. The disposal is expected to generate a pre-tax gain of US$1.8 billion and increase the HSBC Group's CET1 ratio by up to 15 basis points. Following completion, HSBC and Allianz will enter an exclusive 15-year bancassurance distribution agreement, with HSBC receiving an initial S$0.2 billion lump sum payment.
retail_take: Capital-accretive strategic exit from non-core insurance business with retained distribution upside; part of HSBC's simplification strategy focusing on wealth and wholesale banking where it has competitive advantages. Positive read.

headline: High Purity Lithium Carbonate Produced
brief: CleanTech Lithium produces 330kg high-purity lithium carbonate (99.68-99.91%) from pilot plant, advancing Laguna Verde project.
summary: CleanTech Lithium processed 55,000 litres of eluate at Empower's Dallas facility, producing approximately 330kg of high-purity lithium carbonate with purities ranging 99.68-99.91% across five batches, plus 250kg stored in recycle fluids. Concurrent work includes PFS process validation in Chile with Lanshen, DFS-level adsorbent testing in France with Hephaistos, and spent brine reinjection analysis with Zelandez. Results demonstrate the downstream process can consistently produce battery-grade product ahead of definitive feasibility study.
retail_take: Positive technical milestone showing proof-of-concept for Laguna Verde's production pathway with consistently high-purity output, supporting project advancement toward DFS and potential commercialisation in the sought-after Chilean lithium market.

headline: Exercise of Options and Issue of Shares
brief: Block Energy issues 371,657 shares following nil-cost option exercise; total voting rights now 1.47bn.
summary: Block Energy PLC has allotted 371,657 ordinary shares (0.25p each) from exercised nil-cost options granted under a November 2025 capital restructuring. The shares admit to AIM trading on 30 July 2026, ranking pari passu with existing shares. Post-admission, the company will have 1,469,751,612 total voting rights with no treasury shares held.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of option exercise and share issuance; minimal dilution from nil-cost grant vesting. Likely non-event for investors absent broader context around management incentive structures.

headline: Trading Update, Board Change and Notice of Results
brief: Nexteq H1 revenue down 34% to $26.7m due to gaming division weakness; founder retiring August 2026.
summary: H1 2026 revenue expected at $26.7m versus $40.7m prior year, with Densitron displays stable at $13.9m but Quixant gaming halved to $12.7m from $26.9m, hit by North American market tariffs, component cost inflation, and major customer consolidation. Cash fell to $10.7m from $28.5m due to $8.5m shareholder returns and strategic inventory investment. Board expects H2 recovery with traditional seasonal weighting and full-year guidance unchanged. Nicholas Jarmany, non-executive deputy chair and co-founder, retiring 31 August 2026 after leading company since 2005 inception.
retail_take: Market likely negative: 34% revenue decline and halved gaming division significantly outweigh stable displays and management confidence in 2027 recovery. Founder exit removes experienced voice despite positive messaging.

headline: Notification of Results
brief: Mobico to report 15-month results on 29 July with dual investor presentations scheduled.
summary: Mobico Group PLC will release audited results for the 15-month period ending 31 March 2026 on Wednesday, 29 July 2026. Executive Chair Phil White, Group CEO Paco Iglesias, and Group CFO Brian Egan will present to institutional investors and analysts at 09:00am BST, followed by a retail investor webcast via Investor Meet Company at 10:15am BST. Both sessions will allow pre-submission and live questions.
retail_take: Routine results announcement with standard dual-track investor engagement; market reaction will depend on actual financial performance disclosed on 29 July rather than this notification.

headline: Notice of Final Results and Investor Presentation
brief: Zoo Digital to report FY2026 results on 30 July with separate analyst and investor presentations scheduled.
summary: Zoo Digital Group PLC will release final results for the year ended 31 March 2026 on 30 July 2026. Stuart Green (CEO) and Robert Pursell (CFO) will host an analyst presentation at 10:00 BST followed by a private investor presentation via Investor Meet Company at 17:00 BST on the same day. The localisation and digital media services company serves major clients including Disney, Netflix, NBCUniversal and Paramount Global, operating from 10 global hubs with 12,000+ freelancers.
retail_take: Routine results announcement with standard investor engagement; market reaction will depend entirely on reported financial performance, revenue trends and guidance rather than the presentation format itself.

headline: Change of Director Responsibilities
brief: David Wassong steps down as Quantum-appointed director after shareholder stake falls below 10% threshold.
summary: David Wassong has ceased his role as shareholder-appointed director of Quantum Strategic Partners Ltd following termination of the Shareholders' Agreement when Quantum's shareholding in Helios Towers fell below 10%. Wassong remains as non-executive director and was appointed to the Nomination and Technology Committees effective 23 July 2026. The change reflects a routine governance adjustment tied to shareholding levels outlined in the company's 2025 Annual Report.
retail_take: Minor governance reshuffle with no operational impact; Wassong retains board presence in expanded committee roles, suggesting orderly transition rather than conflict or concern.

headline: Hostelworld Group plc Board and Committee Changes
brief: Hostelworld replaces two retiring non-execs with Karen Myers and Wais Shaifta, refreshing board ahead of growth phase.
summary: Éimear Moloney and Carl G. Shepherd step down from the board effective 23 July 2026 after nine years of service. Karen Myers, former Group HR Director at Mobico Group and Chief HR Officer at William Hill, joins as independent non-exec and Remuneration Committee Chair. Wais Shaifta, former Chief Growth Officer at The Co-operative Group, joins as non-exec and succeeds Shepherd as Senior Independent Director. Committee roles also reallocated, with Paul Duffy becoming Audit Committee Chair.
retail_take: Planned succession with experienced hires signals governance stability and strategic continuity. Myers' HR expertise supports upcoming remuneration framework redesign; Shaifta's growth/digital background aligns with management's stated growth ambitions.

headline: Dispute with Smith Square Partners LLP
brief: Home REIT facing £1.6m dispute with former strategic adviser Smith Square Partners over alleged unpaid tail fee.
summary: Smith Square Partners LLP, appointed in early 2023 and terminated November 2023, has issued proceedings claiming £1,623,600 plus contractual interest and costs. The firm alleges Home REIT's November 2025 RNS regarding portfolio sales triggered a 24-month tail fee obligation. Home REIT announced disposal of 706 properties to Patron Capital in March 2026 and intends to vigorously defend the claim.
retail_take: Likely a non-event; dispute concerns contractual interpretation between advisers post-transaction, immaterial to ongoing business given near-completion of portfolio sale to Patron Capital.

headline: Directorate change
brief: Senior Independent Director Karen Whitworth stepping down 31 August 2026 after six years; Keith Laslop becomes interim Audit Committee Chair.
summary: Karen Whitworth, Senior Independent Director since January 2022, is departing the Rank Board effective 31 August 2026 to focus on other commitments. She has served since November 2019 and led recruitment of Chair John Ott and supported CEO Richard Harris's appointment. Keith Laslop, a Non-Executive Director and Audit Committee member since September 2023, will become interim Audit Committee Chair from 1 September 2026. An independent search for Whitworth's replacement has commenced.
retail_take: Orderly succession planning with interim arrangements in place; departure follows recent successful executive appointments, suggesting governance stability rather than concerns.

headline: Directorate change
brief: Victrex reshapes leadership with CFO departure and executive committee changes amid growth strategy shift.
summary: Ian Melling steps down as CFO and board member on 31 July 2026, with interim finance leadership to follow. Four executives depart 31 July: Michael Koch (MD Sustainable Solutions), Jane Brisley (General Counsel), and Andrew Hanson (Investor Relations/ESG). New appointments include Andrew Ng as Chief Commercial Officer (May 2026) and Daniel Diffenderfer as MD Medical. Maria Antoniou joins the board as Non-Executive Director on 1 September, succeeding Janet Ashdown as Remuneration Committee chair on 1 October. Janet Ashdown and Brendan Connolly retire at February 2027 AGM after nine years' service. CEO James Routh (7 months in role) will present strategy at 24 September Capital Markets Event.
retail_take: Significant leadership turnover under new CEO could signal strategic repositioning, but lack of interim CFO details and multiple departures create near-term uncertainty and governance questions for investors to monitor.

headline: Investment Manager’s Year End Review
brief: Ruffer Investment Company reports 4.6% NAV return for year ended 30 June 2026, missing its 8% target despite navigating geopolitical shocks and AI market concentration.
summary: The closed-ended fund delivered 5.5% share price total return and 4.6% NAV total return over 12 months to 30 June 2026. Equity exposure (+4.6%), gold/precious metals (+3.9%), and commodities (+0.6%) drove gains, while credit/derivative strategies (-3.5%) and yen exposure (-2.0%) detracted. The fund missed its objective of twice the Bank of England base rate (8.0% required). Portfolio positioning balances growth opportunities in undervalued markets (UK, China, Japan) with AI exposure while maintaining protection through credit strategies, duration, and inflation-sensitive assets against geopolitical and market concentration risks.
retail_take: Mixed signal: solid capital preservation through volatile period but underperformance versus stated target weakens narrative. All-weather positioning attractive to risk-averse investors but headline miss may concern yield-seekers expecting above-target returns from this strategy.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Charles Wigoder's holding in Telecom Plus rises to 12% due to company's share buyback reducing total voting rights.
summary: Charles Wigoder's stake in Telecom Plus PLC increased from 11.4% to 12.0% of voting rights as of 23 July 2026, following the company's ongoing share buyback programme announced 23 June 2026. Wigoder holds 6,289,728 shares beneficially (8.04%) and 3,092,683 shares non-beneficially through the Wigoder Family Foundation (3.96%), totalling 9,382,411 voting rights. The threshold crossing resulted from reduction in the company's total voting rights due to the buyback, not from additional share purchases.
retail_take: Routine disclosure triggered by mechanical increase in shareholding percentage from buyback-induced voting rights dilution; no material change in actual position or control, standard capital management activity.

headline: Issue of Equity and TVR

summary: Rockwood Strategic PLC agreed on 23 July 2026 to issue 35,000 ordinary shares of 5p each at 317.47p per share from its blocklisting facility, settling 27 July 2026. The issuance will raise approximately £111m gross. Post-issue, the company will have 60,903,760 ordinary shares in issue with no treasury shares, giving a total voting rights denominator of 60,903,760 shares for FCA disclosure purposes.
retail_take: Small equity raise relative to existing capital base; routine use of blocklisting facility signals modest capital need rather than major strategic development or distress.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

The shares will be held in treasury. Post-purchase, total issued capital stands at 33,897,954 shares with 1,453,113 in treasury, leaving 32,444,841 voting shares outstanding.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks typically signal management confidence but this modest purchase (0.04% of outstanding shares) is unlikely to materially impact share price or perception.

Shares held at treasury under previously announced buyback programme.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity. Modest daily purchase within established buyback programme; no material change to shareholding structure or strategy signalled.

retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under an existing programme; modest scale suggests capital management rather than signalling confidence. Non-event unless buybacks accelerate or price rises materially.

retail_take: Routine share buyback signalling capital management and potential shareholder value accretion through reduced share count; non-event absent broader strategic context.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

retail_take: Routine share buyback reducing share count; typical capital management activity for closed-ended funds with minimal direct investor impact.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

retail_take: Routine share cancellation under existing buyback programme announced January 2026. Buyback ongoing during offer period; modest daily reduction in share count supports EPS, but amounts are immaterial to near-term sentiment.

headline: Trading Update
brief: discoverIE reports strong Q1 momentum with orders up 31% organically and sales up 6%, maintaining positive full-year outlook.
summary: For Q1 FY2027, discoverIE Group achieved 31% organic order growth and 6% organic sales growth with a book-to-bill ratio of 1.15. Including recent acquisitions Trival and Storm, sales rose 10% on a constant exchange rate basis. The 3Gmetalworx acquisition remains under regulatory review. Management expects full-year adjusted earnings to track ahead of Board expectations, supported by a strong order book and pipeline of design wins.
retail_take: Market likely to view positively: strong organic orders growth and elevated book-to-bill indicate robust demand, while ahead-of-expectations earnings guidance and acquisition momentum suggest management confidence. FX headwinds partially offset gains.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

retail_take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks typically support EPS accretion and signal management confidence in valuation, though modest scale suggests measured execution rather than aggressive return of capital.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: J Sainsbury plc purchased 3,861,416 ordinary shares via Shore Capital Stockbrokers from 17-23 July 2026 under its existing buyback programme authorised 23 April 2026. retail_take: Routine buyback execution under pre-approved programme; share cancellation reduces count, potentially accretive to EPS. Standard capital management activity with no strategic implications.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

This transaction continues the share buyback programme announced 9 February and commenced 16 February 2026.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution under previously announced programme; modest share count reduction supports per-share metrics but provides no new strategic insight or market signal.

headline: Half-year Financial Report
brief: Law Debenture reports H1 2026 NAV return of 11.4%, outperforming FTSE All-Share; 6% dividend increase declared.
summary: Law Debenture delivered 11.4% NAV total return in H1 2026, beating the FTSE All-Share Index's 7.2%, with share price total return of 16.2%. First interim dividend increased 6% to 8.875p, extending a 47-year record of maintaining or increasing dividends. James Henderson retires as Joint Portfolio Manager in June 2027, succeeded by Laura Foll, who has managed the portfolio since 2019. Independent Professional Services division grew net revenues 6% to £29.9m, now accounting for 15% of NAV but funding one-third of dividends over ten years. Ongoing charges remain low at 0.54%.
retail_take: Strong relative outperformance, consistent dividend growth, and smooth management succession in resilient business model likely to be viewed positively, though performance is past-dependent and macro backdrop remains uncertain.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: The Renewables Infrastructure Group Limited purchased 265,000 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 75.00p through Investec Bank plc, as part of its share buyback programme announced in August 2024. The shares will be held in treasury. Following this purchase, TRIG holds 154,502,374 treasury shares, with 2,331,460,512 voting rights outstanding excluding treasury shares.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution consistent with announced programme; signals capital management but no material impact on fundamentals or valuation outlook for retail investors.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: Pantheon International PLC purchased 175,000 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 via Investec Bank at prices ranging from 387.00p to 389.00p, with a weighted average price of 388.94p. The company intends to cancel these shares. Following the transaction, 393,538,764 ordinary shares remain in issue with total voting rights of 393,538,764.
retail_take: Share buyback with cancellation reduces share count and can support earnings per share accretion; modest positive signal of capital management confidence.

The shares will be held in treasury. Following the purchase, the company has 40,652,593 shares in issue (excluding treasury) and holds 5,533,281 treasury shares.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution with minimal market impact; demonstrates capital allocation strategy but immaterial share count reduction.

summary: International Workplace Group plc purchased 151,816 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 at volume-weighted average price of £1.9683 across multiple venues. Shares will be cancelled or held in treasury. Repurchase conducted by Jefferies International Limited across LSE and alternative venues (Turquoise, CBOE, Aquis).
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within authorised programme; signals management capital allocation policy but no material strategic implications. Market likely views as non-event given buyback was pre-announced.

headline: Publication of Annual Report and Notice of AGM
brief: Mercia Asset Management publishes FY26 results and AGM notice; AuM grew to £2.2bn, EBITDA up to £8.1m.
summary: Mercia Asset Management (AIM: MERC) published its Annual Report for year ended 31 March 2026, with Assets Under Management rising to £2.2bn from £2.0bn. Revenue declined slightly to £34.1m from £34.4m, whilst EBITDA improved to £8.1m from £7.6m. Net Assets fell to £173.6m from £187.9m, and cash decreased to £26.4m from £40.1m. NAV per share fell to 40.8p from 43.6p. Total dividends increased to 1.00p per share from 0.95p. The 2026 AGM is scheduled for 24 September 2026.
retail_take: Mixed signals: AuM growth and EBITDA expansion are positive, but declining revenue, net assets and cash position alongside falling NAV per share warrant scrutiny. Modest dividend increase suggests confidence despite headwinds.

headline: Trading Statement
brief: Renishaw posts record Q4 revenue of £243m; FY2026 profit significantly ahead of expectations at 31% increase.
summary: Renishaw delivered record Q4 revenue of approximately £243m, up 27% year-on-year and 18% sequentially. Full year FY2026 revenue expected at £815m, 14% higher than prior year, with all three segments showing growth. FY2026 adjusted operating profit forecast at £152m and adjusted profit before tax at £167m, representing 31% growth above the prior year, driven by strong demand from semiconductor, electronics, aerospace and defence sectors.
retail_take: Strong operational momentum with significant profit outperformance signals robust end-market demand and execution; likely to be read positively ahead of full results on 23 September.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Celebrus Technologies repurchased 35,000 shares at 108.45p per share on 23 July 2026, holding them in treasury.
summary: Celebrus Technologies PLC purchased 35,000 ordinary shares (2p each) at a volume-weighted average price of 108.45p through Cavendish Capital Markets on 23 July 2026. The shares will be held in treasury. Following this transaction, the company has 40,431,453 shares in issue with 2,625,629 held in treasury, resulting in 37,805,824 voting rights. This figure is used for FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules calculations.
retail_take: Routine share buyback activity signalling capital allocation and potential EPS accretion; standard treasury management with no immediate market read-through unless part of broader shareholder return programme.

summary: Oakley Capital Investments Limited purchased 35,000 ordinary shares at 512 pence per share for cancellation on 23 July 2026, in line with its stated capital allocation policy. retail_take: Routine capital management action; share buybacks for cancellation typically indicate management confidence but carry minimal immediate market implications for a closed-ended investment fund.

summary: AEP Plantations purchased 56,497 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 at prices between 173p and 177p, with a volume-weighted average of 175.35p, through Cavendish Capital Markets. Post-purchase, 382,956,691 voting shares remain in issue (excluding 16.8m treasury shares).
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks typically neutral or slightly positive as they can reduce share count and support EPS, though the market read depends on whether shares are undervalued at current prices.

headline: H1 2026 Trading Update & Notice of H1 2026 Report
brief: Team Internet H1 2026 results in line with expectations; Search returns to profit, DIS and Comparison grow strongly.
summary: Team Internet reported H1 2026 gross revenue of USD 179.1m (down 32% YoY) but improved gross margin to 34.1% from 27.6%. Adjusted EBITDA of USD 19.5m was ahead of H2 2025's USD 18.0m despite legacy AdSense decline. DIS grew adjusted EBITDA 28% YoY, Comparison grew 54% YoY, and Search returned to June profitability. Net debt increased to USD 117.5m from USD 87.6m due to tax settlements and working capital moves. Board expects significant second-half deleverage and year-on-year earnings growth H2 2026. Strategic review of DIS division continues with transaction expected during 2026.
retail_take: Mixed read: operational momentum in core segments and Search turnaround are encouraging, but revenue declines and rising net debt may concern investors; second-half weighting and strategic review provide near-term catalysts but uncertainty remains.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Greencoat Renewables buys back 322,778 shares at €0.76-€0.78 on 23 July as part of announced programme.
summary: Greencoat Renewables purchased 322,778 ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin on 23 July 2026 at prices ranging from €0.7600 to €0.7750, with a volume-weighted average price of €0.7685. retail_take: Routine capital management execution; share buybacks typically neutral or modestly supportive to shareholders through reduced share count, though market perception depends on whether prices paid are viewed as attractive.

retail_take: Routine capital management execution; share buybacks are typically neutral-to-slightly-positive as they support EPS and signal management confidence, though market reaction depends on broader performance context.

summary: InterContinental Hotels Group purchased 1,000 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 through Goldman Sachs International on the London Stock Exchange at prices ranging from $152.45 to $154.65, with a volume-weighted average of $153.3945. retail_take: Routine share buyback execution; immaterial single-day purchase representing a standard capital allocation activity with minimal market impact given the small volume relative to outstanding shares.

summary: B.P. Marsh & Partners PLC purchased 10,000 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 at 680.0 pence per share, to be held in treasury. The transaction was executed on AIM at 16:24:38.
retail_take: Routine treasury share purchase under existing buyback programme; suggests management confidence but minimal immediate market impact for retail investors absent broader strategic context.

headline: Divestment of Russia Hygiene Business
brief: Reckitt divests Russian Hygiene business to Arnest Management; ~1% of revenue; expected £175m post-tax loss.
summary: Reckitt has agreed to sell its Russia Hygiene business to Russian manufacturer Arnest Management LLC, representing approximately 1% of Core Reckitt's 2025 net revenue. The transaction, expected to complete in H2 2026 subject to UK regulatory approval, will result in a post-tax loss of approximately £175 million (£125m in H1 2026). Around 400 local employees will transfer to Arnest. International sanctions have impacted the Russia Hygiene business by approximately 200 basis points on Emerging Markets revenue in Q1 2026, with similar headwinds expected until completion. Reckitt retains its Russia Health business and expects no material impact on 2026 Group Adjusted Operating Profit or Adjusted EPS.
retail_take: Market likely views this as a non-event strategically—divesting a 1% revenue contributor with heavy sanctions headwinds while ringfencing the loss. No material impact to full-year guidance suggests well-managed exit.

headline: Extension of Options
brief: SRT Marine extends executive share options by two years to 2028, affecting CEO, CFO and COO holdings.
summary: SRT Marine Systems has extended the exercise period for share options granted to three executives: CEO Simon Tucker (1.5m options), CFO Richard Hurd (450k options), and COO Neil Peniket (250k options). All options carry a 0.1p exercise price and were originally granted 8 August 2016, with expiry extended from 8 August 2026 to 8 August 2028. The remuneration committee approved the extension to minimise shareholder dilution while maintaining executive incentivisation aligned with shareholder interests.
retail_take: Routine governance adjustment; extension of underwater options (0.1p strike) suggests management confidence but minimal immediate dilution risk if share price remains elevated. Non-event for most investors.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Gaming Realms repurchases 66,815 shares at 30.20-30.70p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Gaming Realms PLC purchased 66,815 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 at prices between 30.20p and 30.70p (VWAP 30.52p) via Peel Hunt. Shares will be held in treasury. The company now holds 24.6m treasury shares with 271.7m shares in issue, representing total voting rights.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution; demonstrates capital allocation confidence but provides limited new information or insight into operational performance or strategy.

headline: Result of Retail Offer
brief: Investment Company PLC retail offer raises £299k from 391,942 shares at 76.41p; admission 28 July.
summary: Investment Company PLC's retail offer, launched 15 July 2026, received commitments totalling £299,482.88 for 391,942 new ordinary shares at 76.41 pence per share. Combined with a separate institutional placing and offer for subscription raising approximately £7.16m, the total fundraising is approximately £7.46m. New shares admit to the Main Market on 28 July 2026. Proceeds will be invested in accordance with the company's revised investment objective focusing on UK small and mid-cap quoted companies, managed by Chelverton Asset Management.
retail_take: Modest retail participation (£299k of ~£7.46m total) suggests institutional appetite drives the raise; admission timing clear; no material red flags for existing shareholders.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Grafton Group purchased 60,000 shares on 23 July 2026 at average price of £9.47 as part of £25m buyback programme.
summary: Grafton Group plc repurchased 60,000 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 through Goodbody Stockbrokers at a volume-weighted average price of £9.4673, with prices ranging from £9.3200 to £9.5990. This forms part of the £25m share buyback programme announced on 30 June 2026. To date, 1,079,172 shares have been purchased for cancellation through Goodbody and Deutsche Bank since the programme commenced.
retail_take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and commitment to shareholder returns, though market reaction depends on broader economic context and whether this represents optimal capital allocation versus organic investment.

headline: Geophysical Survey Results & Investor Presentation
brief: Arc Minerals extends prospective copper contact to 18km in Botswana; drilling commences early August.
summary: Arc Minerals' geophysical survey over licence PL135/2017 in Botswana's Kalahari Copper Belt extended the inferred D'Kar-Ngwako Pan formation contact to 18km—four times longer than previously expected. The 59 line km IP survey identified multiple high-priority anomalies with strong chargeability/resistivity signatures and structural complexity suitable for mineral traps. Drilling contractor selection is imminent with activities expected in early August. The project lies adjacent to MMG's Khoemacau zone discoveries and within 20km of the Zone 5 deposit (92Mt at 2.2% Cu).
retail_take: Positive early-stage signal: extended prospective contact and imminent drilling could validate exploration thesis, but results remain speculative pre-drill. Market likely views favourably pending actual intersections.

headline: Site Access Agreement & Implementation Assessment
brief: Neo Energy gains site access to New Beisa project while regulatory mining right transfer continues; £3.2m assessment programme underway.
summary: Neo Energy's 70%-owned subsidiary signed a site access agreement with Sibanye-Stillwater, granting access to Beatrix 4 Shaft Mining Area (New Beisa Node) to begin a fully-funded £3.2m assessment programme. Three workstreams underway covering gold plant refurbishment, uranium processing metallurgical study, and site infrastructure assessment, expected to complete in eight months. Regulatory transfer under MPRDA Section 11 deadline extended to 6 December 2026 (Phase 1) and 6 June 2027 (Phase 2). First gold production remains targeted for December 2027.
retail_take: Positive momentum: company moves to operational readiness while regulatory process continues, de-risking the timeline and demonstrating Sibanye's continued backing through support during interim period.

headline: Teufelskuppe – New Drill Results
brief: Kendrick Resources reports strong rare earth element drill results from Teufelskuppe project in Namibia, confirming high-grade shallow mineralisation.
summary: Diamond drill holes TKDD007-TKDD010 at the Teufelskuppe carbonatite complex returned consistent light rare earth oxide (LREO) grades of 2.5-3.5 wt% over substantial widths. TKDD010 delivered 3.15 wt% LREO over 15m and 2.50 wt% over 26m; TKDD009 returned 3.25 wt% over 17m including 4.04 wt% over 9.5m. Three drilling rigs continue operations to generate data for a mineral resource estimate. The company is pursuing JORC-2012 certification of a provisional 14Mt surface resource announced May 2026.
retail_take: Consistent positive drill results with excellent grades and continuity across multiple holes reinforce project quality and de-risk resource estimates; likely to support investor confidence in the Zambian/Namibian rare earths play given strong global demand backdrop.

headline: Partnership Agreement with Nexara Capital
brief: RC365's RCPAY subsidiary enters strategic partnership with Hong Kong-based Nexara Capital for virtual banking and fintech services.
summary: RCPAY Limited has signed a partnership agreement with Nexara Capital Limited (Hong Kong) effective 22 July 2026, establishing a framework for multi-currency virtual banking accounts, API integration, and wealth management SaaS services. The agreement has an initial one-year term with automatic renewal. Nexara, whose subsidiaries hold SFC licenses for securities dealing, advising, and asset management, will adopt RCPAY's RC3.0 Virtual Account solution. The partnership demonstrates RC365's RC3.0 B2B2C model application in the wealth-management sector and supports the group's Asian expansion strategy.
retail_take: Market likely views this positively as evidence of RC365's commercial traction in Asia and sector diversification beyond e-commerce, though actual revenue impact remains contingent on service rollout and execution.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: GCP Infrastructure repurchased 250,000 shares at 83.10p average price on 23 July 2026.
summary: GCP Infrastructure Investments Ltd purchased 250,000 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 at a volume-weighted average price of 83.10p, with a range of 82.40p to 83.40p. The transaction was executed through Canaccord Genuity under shareholder authority from the February 2026 AGM. Since the buyback programme launch in December 2024, the company has repurchased 80.4 million shares for treasury, reducing voting rights to 787.5 million shares.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within established programme; demonstrates capital management and potential support for NAV per share, though buybacks at 83p suggest shares trading near or above recent valuations.

headline: AGM Statement
brief: Truetide seeks shareholder approval to shift from traditional investing to balanced long-short AI-focused strategy with put options.
summary: Truetide plc will seek shareholder approval at its AGM to amend its investing policy to adopt a balanced long-short, AI-focused investment strategy. The strategy targets companies positioned to benefit from artificial intelligence developments, with selective put option positions in overvalued companies. Implementation will proceed in phases following shareholder approval, with full details in a shareholder circular expected within two months.
retail_take: Strategic pivot to AI exposure with downside hedging signals active repositioning; market reception depends on confidence in execution and current valuation context.

headline: West Coast Silver Quarterly Activities Report
brief: Joint venture partner West Coast Silver reports significant high-grade silver drilling results at Elizabeth Hill project in Western Australia.
summary: West Coast Silver's Q2 2026 activities show strong progress at Elizabeth Hill Silver Project. Diamond drilling intersected 3m at 524 g/t silver with peak of 1,039 g/t, defining a 50m north-south high-grade zone beneath historical workings. RC drilling extended near-surface mineralisation across 44-60m intervals at 23-29 g/t silver. Initial JORC resource stands at 141,000 tonnes at 617 g/t silver (2.795 Moz). Alien holds 30% project interest and 30.5m WCS shares (8.7% stake). A$3m cash balance supports planned resource expansion and metallurgical work.
retail_take: Positive for Alien as joint venture partner; high-grade results and resource definition support Elizabeth Hill value creation, enhancing Alien's indirect equity exposure through WCS shareholding.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: SThree plc purchased 28,972 ordinary shares through Investec Bank on 21 July 2026 at a volume-weighted average price of 211.26 pence per share, with trades ranging from 203.5p to 214.5p. The shares will be cancelled. The buyback was executed across 88 individual transactions throughout the trading day, with the largest single trade comprising 1,058 shares at 214.5p.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution with purchased shares to be cancelled; supports capital structure management but provides no material operational insight.

headline: Dividend Declaration
brief: CGI declares quarterly dividend of $0.31/share, up 14.8% YoY, payable September 15, 2026.
summary: Canadian General Investments Limited declared a quarterly dividend of $0.31 per share payable September 15, 2026 to shareholders of record August 31, 2026. The dividend represents a 14.8% increase from Q3 2025 and is designated as a capital gains dividend under Canadian tax law. CGI, a closed-end equity fund focused on Canadian investments established in 1930, continues its multi-decade track record of sustainable growing dividends.
retail_take: Strong dividend growth signals management confidence in portfolio performance and capital gains realization; likely viewed positively by income-focused investors, though offset by recent share price weakness (-$0.20 on announcement day).

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Avation repurchased 15,000 shares at 136.5p under its approved buyback programme.
summary: Aircraft leasing company Avation PLC repurchased 15,000 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 at 136.5 pence per share through the market. Following the transaction, 61,425,369 shares remain in issue with 1,625,068 held in treasury, reducing total voting rights to 59,800,301. The buyback was conducted under the company's approved share repurchase programme authorising up to 25% of issued share capital, mandated at the November 2025 AGM.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; buyback continues under existing authority with no material implications for shareholder value or market positioning in the near term.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: Between 17-23 July 2026, Young & Co's Brewery repurchased 11,716 non-voting shares at an average price of 730.16p and 7,000 A shares at 901.00p under its buyback programme announced 8 July. Non-voting shares will be cancelled; A shares held in treasury. Post-purchase, 22.56m non-voting shares and 38.03m A shares remain in issue.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution under announced programme; modest capital return to shareholders. Non-event for market—standard treasury management activity with no material strategic implications.

headline: NBPE Announces Transaction in Own Shares
brief: NBPE repurchases 30,000 Class A shares at £14.50-£14.66, to be cancelled.
summary: Neuberger Private Equity Partners Limited purchased 30,000 Class A Shares on 23 July 2026 via Jefferies International Limited at prices between £14.50 and £14.66 per share, exercising shareholder-approved buyback authority granted 11 June 2026. All repurchased shares will be cancelled. Outstanding Class A Shares post-cancellation: 40,459,301, with an additional 3,150,408 held in treasury.
retail_take: Routine capital management action reducing share count; modest scale suggests capital-efficient deployment rather than significant shareholder return signal.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: PSH completes share buyback of 33,465 shares at average price of 49.65 USD on 23 July 2026.
summary: Pershing Square Holdings repurchased 33,465 public shares on 23 July 2026 at an average price of 49.65 USD (3,731 pence), with 22,964 shares bought on LSE (PSH ticker) and 10,501 on PSHD ticker. The buyback was executed by Jefferies at NAV of 76.01 USD per share. Post-buyback, PSH has 173.6 million public shares outstanding with 37.3 million held in treasury.
retail_take: Routine share buyback at discount to NAV (49.65 USD vs 76.01 USD) accretive to remaining shareholders; standard capital management activity with neutral-to-modest positive read from execution below valuation.

headline: FARON PHARMACEUTICALS LTD: HOLDING(S) IN COMPANY
brief: Heights Capital Management increases stake in Faron Pharmaceuticals to 20.52% through direct shares and convertible bonds.
summary: Heights Capital Management, Inc. (via CVI Investments, Inc.) crossed a notifiable threshold in Faron Pharmaceuticals OY on 22 July 2026, building a total position of 20.52% of voting rights. The stake comprises 46.8 million direct voting rights (20.52%) and 28.6 million potential voting rights from convertible bonds expiring February 2027 and 2028 (12.53%). This represents a decrease from the previous notification position of 21.41%, indicating a modest reduction in overall control through conversion instrument holdings.
retail_take: Routine major shareholding disclosure showing a significant investor maintaining substantial influence in the biotech firm; the slight overall decrease suggests consolidation rather than aggressive expansion, likely a non-event for markets.

headline: Pershing Square Holdings, Ltd. to Publish its Semiannual Financial Statements on August 13, 2026
brief: PSH to release H1 2026 financial statements and host investor Q&A on August 13, 2026.
summary: Pershing Square Holdings will publish its 2026 semiannual financial statements on August 13, 2026, including the Investment Manager's quarterly portfolio review. CEO Bill Ackman and CIO Ryan Israel will then conduct a live Q&A on X at 3:00 p.m. BST (10:00 a.m. ET), open to investors, media and the public, with replay available via the investor relations website.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of scheduled financial results publication; the live Q&A adds engagement value but represents standard investor relations activity with no material news implications.
